wikiautomator-intro: Welcome to WikiAutomator. Manage your automated tasks below.
wikiautomator-trigger-page-create: Event: When new page is created
wikiautomator-scheduled-time-help: Task will run once at specified time and then auto-disable (at least 5 minutes from now)
wikiautomator-condition-title-help: Leave empty to monitor all pages.
wikiautomator-condition-ns-help: Enter namespace ID (e.g., 0=Main, 2=User). Leave empty for all namespaces.
wikiautomator-condition-ns-none-hint: Leave unchecked to allow all namespaces.
wikiautomator-edit-summary-help: Leave empty to auto-generate summary (shows action type). This will appear in Recent Changes.
wikiautomator-error-no-steps: Error: Please configure at least one execution step.
wikiautomator-error-no-category: Error: Category change trigger requires a category name.
wikiautomator-error-no-scheduled-time: Error: Scheduled execution requires a time to be set (year, month, day are required).
wikiautomator-error-scheduled-time-futur: Error: Scheduled time must be at least 5 minutes in the future.
wikiautomator-error-invalid-date: Error: Invalid date. Please check year, month, and day.
wikiautomator-error-invalid-time: Error: Invalid time. Hour (0-23), minute (0-59), second (0-59).
wikiautomator-error-invalid-time-format: Error: Execution time format is incorrect.
wikiautomator-error-permission-or-missin: Insufficient permission or task does not exist
wikiautomator-warning-blank-replacement: Warning: Replacement is empty. This will delete all matched text.
wikiautomator-warning-replacement-exists: Warning: The replacement string already exists in some pages. Undoing may be difficult.
wikiautomator-bot-edit-help: When checked, edits will be marked as bot edits in Recent Changes (hidden by default).
wikiautomator-confirm-delete-step: Are you sure you want to delete this step?
wikiautomator-empty-steps: No execution steps yet. Click "Add Step" to start configuring.
wikiautomator-step-replace-value-placeho: Replacement text (leave empty to delete matches)
wikiautomator-step-search-warning: Each trigger will perform a site-wide search, which may affect performance. Consider using scheduled or manual execution instead.
wikiautomator-trigger-option-page-create: Event: When new page is created
wikiautomator-scheduled-time-help-full: Task will run once at specified time and then auto-disable (at least 5 minutes from now). Timezone can be configured via $wgWikiAutomatorTimezone, default is Asia/Shanghai.
wikiautomator-condition-title-help-text: Leave empty to not limit trigger pages.
wikiautomator-session-expired: Session expired or invalid request. Please try again.
wikiautomator-delete-failed: Cannot delete: insufficient permission or task does not exist.
wikiautomator-task-not-exist-or-denied: Task does not exist or permission denied.
wikiautomator-task-config-invalid-action: Task configuration error: invalid action data.
wikiautomator-preview-trigger-page-place: Enter page name to simulate trigger (for resolving {{PAGENAME}})
wikiautomator-preview-no-results: This task has no execution steps, or no matching content was found.
wikiautomator-preview-submitted: Task "$1" has been submitted, $2 item(s) selected. The task will be processed in the background.
wikiautomator-undo-cannot: This execution record cannot be undone (status: $1).
wikiautomator-undo-warning: About to undo the following execution:
wikiautomator-undo-revert-intro: The following pages will be reverted to the version before WikiAutomator edit:
wikiautomator-run-no-change: Task execution completed, but no changes may have been made.
wikiautomator-run-failed: Execution failed. Please check the log for details.
wikiautomator-run-permission-denied: Cannot execute: insufficient permission or task does not exist.
wikiautomator-tasklist-confirm-delete: Are you sure you want to delete task #$1?
wikiautomator-email-intro: Your WikiAutomator task on $1 has been executed.
wikiautomator-email-footer:
This email was sent automatically by the WikiAutomator extension on $1.
To modify task settings, please visit the Special:WikiAutomator page.
wikiautomator-help-content: == Quick Start == The most common scenario: a one-time site-wide text replacement. # Go to Special:WikiAutomator, create a new task, and enter a task name. # Select "Manual execution" as the trigger mode. # Add an execution step: #* Select "Search matching pages" as the target type. #* Select "Find and replace" as the action type. #* Select "Literal text" as the match mode. #* Enter the text to find in the "Find" box and the replacement text in the "Replace with" box. # After saving the task, click "Preview" to see which pages will be affected and the before/after diff. # Once confirmed, select the pages you want to execute on the preview page and click "Execute Selected". To undo, go to [[Special:WikiAutomator/log|Execution Log]] and click the "Undo" button for the corresponding record. == Trigger Modes == WikiAutomator supports the following trigger modes: {| class="wikitable" ! Mode !! Description |- | '''Manual execution''' || Not automatically triggered. Run manually via the "Run" or "Preview" buttons in the task list. Suitable for one-time batch operations. |- | '''Page save''' || Triggered when a page is edited and saved. Can be combined with "Limit to page title" and "Trigger namespace scope" to narrow the trigger range. |- | '''Page create''' || Triggered only when a new page is first created. Editing existing pages will not trigger. |- | '''Category change''' || Triggered when a page is added to or removed from a specified category. Requires a category name (without the Category: prefix). Can choose "add", "remove", or "both". |- | '''Scheduled periodic''' || Execute at custom intervals (minimum 5 minutes). |- | '''Scheduled time''' || One-time task that auto-disables after execution at the specified time. Time must be set at least 5 minutes in the future. |} In event trigger modes (page save/create/category change), step targets can use the {{PAGENAME}} variable to reference the trigger page title (without namespace prefix). == Execution Step Configuration == Each task can have multiple sequential execution steps, each with independent target, action, and match mode settings. === Target Types === Each step has three target page options: {| class="wikitable" ! Type !! Description |- | '''Trigger page''' || Operate on the page that triggered the event. In manual mode, uses the "Limit to page title" condition or the site main page. |- | '''Specific page''' || Manually enter the target page title. Supports {{PAGENAME}} and {{FULLPAGENAME}} variables. |- | '''Search matching pages''' || Search the database for matching pages and batch operate. See [[#Search matching pages|Search matching pages]] section. |} === Action Types === {| class="wikitable" ! Action !! Description |- | '''Append''' || Add content at the end of the page. |- | '''Prepend''' || Insert content at the beginning of the page. |- | '''Overwrite''' || Clear existing content and write new content. |- | '''Replace''' || Find and replace page content. Optionally replace page titles (rename pages). See [[#Replace details|Replace details]] section. |} === Match Modes === Match mode is set independently at each step level, with three options: {| class="wikitable" ! Mode !! Description !! Use Case |- | '''Literal text''' || Exact literal text matching || Simple text replacement |- | '''Wildcard''' || Use * to match any content, optimized for MediaWiki syntax || Templates, links, comments, etc. |- | '''Regular expression''' || Full PCRE regex support, including capture groups and flag options || Complex pattern matching |} == Search Matching Pages == When a step target is set to "Search matching pages", the system searches the database for matching pages and batch operates. This mode is available for all action types. === Replace Action === The system automatically uses the "Find" content as the search keyword to find pages containing that content in the database. === Non-replace Actions (Append/Prepend/Overwrite) === A separate "Search keyword" field must be filled in to specify which pages to search. === Filter Conditions === {| class="wikitable" ! Filter !! Description |- | '''Namespace''' || Limit search scope to specified namespaces (e.g., Main, User, etc.). |- | '''Category''' || Only search pages belonging to the specified category. |- | '''Title prefix''' || Only search pages whose titles start with the specified prefix. |- | '''Page limit''' || Limit the maximum number of search results (default 500). Prevents accidentally affecting too many pages. |} == Replace Details == === Find and Replace Input === The replace action uses two separate input boxes: * '''Find''': The text or pattern to find. * '''Replace with''': The replacement content. Leave empty to delete matched content. === Also Replace Page Titles === When "Also replace page titles" is checked, in addition to replacing page content, the same find/replace will be applied to page titles (i.e., renaming pages). === Match Mode Comparison === {| class="wikitable" ! Scenario !! Recommended Mode !! Find !! Replace with |- | Simple text replacement || Literal text || apple || banana |- | Delete all templates || Wildcard || {{delete*}} || (leave empty) |- | Date format conversion || Regular expression || (\d{4})-(\d{2})-(\d{2}) || $1/$2/$3 |} === Regex Flag Options === When match mode is set to "Regular expression", additional flag options are shown: {| class="wikitable" ! Flag !! Description |- | '''i (case-insensitive)''' || Ignore case when matching. hello matches Hello, HELLO. |- | '''m (multiline)''' || ^ and $ match the start and end of each line, not the entire text. |- | '''U (non-greedy)''' || Quantifiers default to non-greedy matching. .* is equivalent to .*?. |} === Capture Group References === In regex mode, replacement content can use $1, $2, etc. to reference capture groups: {| class="wikitable" ! Find !! Replace !! Effect |- | (\d{4})-(\d{2})-(\d{2}) || $1/$2/$3 || 2025-01-31 → 2025/01/31 |- | (Category):(\w+) || Cat:$2 || Category:Test → Cat:Test |- | \{\{[Ss]tub\}\} || {{Stub-article}} || Replace stub/Stub template (braces must be escaped) |} === Empty Replacement and Duplicate Warnings === * If "Replace with" is left empty, a warning is shown (matched content will be deleted). * If the page already contains the replacement content, a warning is also shown to avoid duplicate operations. == Wildcard Syntax == Wildcards are a simplified pattern matching system designed for MediaWiki syntax. Use * to match any content. {| class="wikitable" ! Wildcard Pattern !! Matches !! Example |- | {{*}} || Any template (including nested) || {{TemplateName}}, {{Template|param}} |- | [[*]] || Any link || [[Page]], [[Page|Display text]] |- | || Any HTML comment || |- | * || Any text (non-greedy) || Matches as few characters as possible |} === Wildcard Examples === {| class="wikitable" ! Find !! Replace !! Effect |- | {{outdated*}} || {{updated}} || Replace all outdated templates with updated |- | {{delete*}} || (leave empty) || Delete all delete-related templates |- | [[Category:*]] || (leave empty) || Delete all category tags |- | || (leave empty) || Delete all HTML comments |} '''Recommendation''': Prefer wildcards for templates and links. Only use regex when capture groups or complex matching is needed. == Preview and Selective Execution == Click the "Preview" button in the task list to view without executing: * Which pages will be affected and the match count for each page. * Context snippets of matched content (highlighted). * Before/after diff comparison for replace actions (expandable). * Warnings for empty replacement and existing replacement content. The preview page supports selective execution: * Each affected page has a checkbox to deselect pages you don't want to execute. * A select all/none checkbox at the top. * Click "Execute Selected" to only operate on checked pages. The preview page also provides a "Simulated trigger page" input box to enter a page name to simulate event trigger scenarios, useful for testing {{PAGENAME}} variable resolution. == Execution Log and Undo == === Execution Log === Visit [[Special:WikiAutomator/log]] to view all task execution records, including: * Execution time, task name, executor. * Pages affected and match count. * Execution status (success/partial success/failed/no change/undone). * Expandable details for each affected page. The log page supports filtering by task ID, username, and status. === Undo Feature === In the execution log, records with "success" or "partial success" status can be undone: # Click the "Undo" link on the right side of the record. # The confirmation page lists all pages that will be reverted. # Click "Confirm Undo" to revert each page to the version before the WikiAutomator edit. After undoing, the log status changes to "undone". == Advanced Features == === Multi-step Tasks === A task can have multiple sequential execution steps, executed in order. Each step can independently set target type, action type, and match mode. === Email Notification === Check "Send email notification after execution" to receive an email notification when the task completes. === Mark as Bot Edit === When "Mark as bot edit" is checked, WikiAutomator edits will be marked as bot edits in Recent Changes (hidden by default for regular users). Suitable for reducing noise in Recent Changes during batch operations. === Custom Edit Summary === A custom edit summary can be entered; leave empty to auto-generate. The summary appears in page history and Recent Changes. === Task List Filters === The task list page supports filtering by creator, enabled status, trigger type, and match mode for convenient management of many tasks. === {{PAGENAME}} Variable === In event trigger modes, {{PAGENAME}} is replaced with the trigger page title (without namespace prefix). {{FULLPAGENAME}} includes the namespace prefix. For example, when the trigger page is Help:User Guide: * {{PAGENAME}} → User Guide * {{FULLPAGENAME}} → Help:User Guide == Security Limits == * Minimum interval for scheduled tasks: 5 minutes. * Scheduled time tasks must be set at least 5 minutes in the future and auto-disable after execution. * Search matching pages returns a maximum of 500 results by default, adjustable via the page limit setting. * All edit operations are recorded in the execution log and support undo. * Tasks can only be modified or deleted by their creator and administrators.
